item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I glad a lot that you enjoyed this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this idea. A short, sweet article featuring two armies of morally dispensible antagonists fighting it out for no particular reason makes for very entertaining reading and some very satisfying mental images of orcs being mowed down by cliche Nazis. +1 partially because I would pay ALL the money to see this in movie format

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks you, It's nice that you liked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks a lot, I glad you enjoy it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a sucker for decent, short to medium length SCPs especially if they have images. I have no idea what any of this is suppose to mean or what it's a reference to but I like it. There's a couple of grammar mistakes and odd sentences so I'll patch some of those up for you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this one, although there are a few minor grammatical errors and parts that could be reworded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could take a picture and post it here because the pictures both start directly after the initial words of a paragraph, which is confusing.

No need to apologize, I just thought some harsher readers would downvote potentially.

I would suggest considering changing the presentation of the pictures so they don’t interact with the text so much

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for your reading and observations

Regarding PdI, it's an abbreviation in Spanish for PoI, it was a detail that I accidentally left out during the proofreading and that was already corrected by Uncle Nicolini (Thanks Nico!)

About the format, I wrote this article on my computer, so I chose "standard" dimensions for the images (a width of 350 px for the event image and 220 px for the images of both PoI, images aligned on the right), which looked good. Now I checked it on my cell phone and although the images look somewhat large, I don't think they will interrupt the reading to any great extent, I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

The article says that both entities "are very similar" to both PoI because in universo and in spite of all the signals received, and that the actions would be consistent with the memories of both subjects (already deceased), the members of the Foundation have no way to confirm 100% that they really correspond to them (or to their memories, psychic echoes or their ghosts/spirits)

From a narrative point of view, I think there are times when it is better to hint than to say directly, to give the reader space to give interpretations to what he has read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The formatting in this skip looks wonky on mobile. For example the lead image is all the way to the left, looking like it’s partially cut off. Also both the images of Lee and the German officer split a paragraph in a really awkward way.

I think you shouldn’t say the two PdI’s “closely resemble” Lee and the German officer, if your ultimate point is this is actually Christopher Lee. You should just say it is Lee.

Also, what is a PdI?

item.title: Image sources
item.content:encoded:
The images used in this article are complying with the license requirements and the sources are:

https://unsplash.com/photos/S9NQnIV4zOI Forest after Event Scharmützel 2017

https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/40/Christopher_Lee_KVIFF.jpg Christopher Lee

https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/3/39/Otto_Skorzeny2.jpg Otto Skorzeny

item.title: The greatest performance of an actor
item.content:encoded: A lot of thanks to Cerastes and morhadow for their reading, review, and suggestions for this work. Also, thanks to Zyn for answering my questions via PM.
